paper_focus.title: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
paper_focus.research_objective: To clarify the co-precipitation behavior and mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel.

material_system.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
material_system.subject: Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
material_system.claim: The nominal composition of the studied steel is Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu.
material_system.evidence: The Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-(0,3)Cu austenitic low-density steel (0Cu steel or 3Cu steel) with nominal composition

processing.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
processing.subject: Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-(0,3)Cu steel
processing.claim: The steel was processed by solution treatment at 1000°C for 1 hour and aging treatment at 550°C for 3 hours.
processing.evidence: processed by solution treatment (at 1000℃ for 1 h) and aging treatment (at 550℃ for 3 h) sequentially

structure.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
structure.subject: Cu-rich phase in aged 3Cu steel
structure.claim: The Cu-rich phase crystal plane spacing (002) is 0.182 nm, consistent with FCC Cu.
structure.evidence: Cu-rich phase crystal plane spacing (00 2) is 0.182 nm which is consistent with Cu in FCC structure

structure.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
structure.subject: orientation relationship in aged 3Cu steel
structure.claim: The κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase have a cube-on-cube orientation relationship with austenitic matrix: [110]γ//[110]κ//[110]Cu and (111)γ//(111)κ//(111)Cu, as shown in Fig. 1.
structure.evidence: κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase have a cube-on-cube orientation relationship with the austenitic matrix: [11 0] //[11 0] //[110] and (11 1) //(11 1) //(11 1)

structure.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
structure.subject: Cu-rich phase in aged 3Cu steel
structure.claim: The Cu-rich phase has a BCC structure according to SAED analysis.
structure.evidence: the Cu-rich phase has BCC structure (Fig. 1, inset)

structure.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
structure.subject: Cu-rich phase in aged 3Cu steel
structure.claim: The Cu-rich phase precipitates directly in FCC structure in the austenitic matrix.
structure.evidence: the Cu-rich phase in austenitic precipitates directly in the FCC structure

structure.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
structure.subject: κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase in aged 3Cu steel
structure.claim: The lattice parameter of FCC Cu-rich phase is a = 0.3652 nm and of κ-carbide is a = 0.3775 nm.
structure.evidence: The lattice parameter of FCC Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide are a = 0.3652 nm and a = 0.3775 nm

structure.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
structure.subject: Cu-rich phase in aged 3Cu steel
structure.claim: The Cu-rich phase is mainly irregular oval with a diameter of ~10 nm and a small amount of bar shapes with a length of ~30 nm.
structure.evidence: The Cu-rich phase is mainly irregular oval and has a small amount of bars shapes, which the diameter of irregular oval Cu-rich phase is ~ 10 nm and the length...

structure.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
structure.subject: austenitic matrix in solution-treated 3Cu steel
structure.claim: The lattice parameter of the austenitic matrix under solution treatment is a = 0.3689 nm obtained by XRD.
structure.evidence: The lattice parameter of austenitic matrix under solution treatment is a = 0.3689 nm obtained by XRD

structure.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
structure.subject: interfacial energy of κ-carbide vs Cu-rich phase
structure.claim: The interfacial energy of κ-carbide with austenitic matrix (0.025 J/m^2) is lower than that of Cu-rich phase (0.086 J/m^2), explaining earlier precipitation of κ-carbide during aging.
structure.evidence: The interfacial energy of κ-carbide with austenitic matrix is lower than Cu-rich phase with austenitic matrix. Therefore, in the early stage of aging...

mechanisms.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
mechanisms.subject: κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase co-precipitation
mechanisms.claim: κ-carbide precipitates first, and its precipitation promotes Cu-rich phase precipitation by increasing Cu concentration near κ-carbide.
mechanisms.evidence: κ-carbide precipitated from austenitic matrix, and Cu atoms migrate to the austenitic matrix without κ-carbide, thereby increasing the Cu concentration near...

mechanisms.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
mechanisms.subject: lattice misfit between phases in aged 3Cu steel
mechanisms.claim: The lattice misfit between κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase is 3.4%, between κ-carbide and austenitic matrix is 2.3%, and between Cu-rich phase and austenitic matrix is 1.0%.
mechanisms.evidence: the lattice misfit between κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase is 3.4 %, the lattice misfit between κ-carbide and austenitic matrix is 2.3 %, and the lattice misfit...

mechanisms.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
mechanisms.subject: Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide
mechanisms.claim: Due to lattice misfit between the three phases being less than 5%, they are in a coherent relationship.
mechanisms.evidence: Due to the lattice misfit between the three phases being less than 5 %, the three phases are in a coherent relationship

mechanisms.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
mechanisms.subject: Cu-rich phase precipitation
mechanisms.claim: Cu-rich phase has a lower nucleation energy barrier in austenitic matrix because its lattice misfit with austenitic matrix is lower than that with κ-carbide.
mechanisms.evidence: Cu-rich phase has a lower nucleation energy barrier in austenitic matrix. Cu-rich phase precipitated in austenitic matrix instead of κ-carbide

mechanisms.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
mechanisms.subject: interfacial energy for κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase
mechanisms.claim: The interfacial energy of κ-carbide with austenitic matrix is 0.025 J/m^2 and of Cu-rich phase with austenitic matrix is 0.086 J/m^2, both below 0.1 J/m^2.
mechanisms.evidence: The interfacial energy of κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-Mn-Al-C steel is 0.025 J/m^2 ... The interfacial energy of Cu in austenitic matrix is 0.086 J/m^2

characterization.section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
characterization.subject: aged 3Cu steel
characterization.claim: TEM-EDS mapping shows that κ-carbide (white dashed box) excludes Cu atoms, while Cu-rich phase (magenta line area) excludes Fe, Mn, and Al atoms.
characterization.evidence: TEM-EDS mapping shows that Fe, Mn and Al atoms exhibited brighter contrast within the white dashed box zone, while Cu atoms exhibit darker contrast. However,...
